REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF HOWARD LEE'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE IS PROVIDED BY THE PLAINTIFF FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE THAT THE CLAIM UPON WHICH THE ATTACHMENT IS SOUGHT HAS PROBABLE VALIDITY. IF PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO RE-FILE THIS MOTION THE PLAINTIFF MUST INCLUDE A COPY OF THE 2006 AND 2007 CONTRACT WITH THE DEFENDANTS OR STATE THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT VERBATIM, INCLUDING THE OBLIGATIONS OF THE DEFENDANTS UNDER THESE CONTRACTS. PLAINTIFF MUST FURTHER PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF COMPLIED WITH HIS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Feb-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF HOWARD LEE'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is DENIED without prejudice. Insufficient evidence is provided by the Plaintiff for the court to determine that the claim upon which the attachment is sought has probable validity. If Plaintiff intends to re-file this motion the Plaintiff must include a copy of the 2006 and 2007 contract with the defendants or state the terms of the contract verbatim, including the obligations of the defendants under these contracts. Plaintiff must further present evidence that Plaintiff complied with his obligations under the contract.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendants Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Stephen G. Opperwall
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Stephen G. Opperwall, ESq., LAW OFFICES OF STEPHEN G. OPPERWALL, (925) 417-0300, steve.opperwallCacomcast.net, for Plaintiff Howard Lee (via Zoom Conference)
Jonathan Ryan Tung, Esq., 408.359.7311, blacksheeplawyer@gmail.com, for Defendants Yessica Cai, et al, (In-Person Appearance)
Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFF HOWARD LEE'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendants Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment

Plaintiff's "motion for reconsideration of order granting defendants' motion to set aside default and default judgment" is denied. A reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) Those requirements are not satisfied here.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Or Default Default And Default Judgment, If Entered Under Ccp 473 (B), Ccp 473.5, Ccp 473(D) For Extrinsic Fraud Or Mistake And Quash Service Of Summons (Ccp 418.10); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Yseeica Cai, Declaration Of Carmencita "Cj" Jocson, Declaration Of Yifeng Chen
Off Calendar Jan-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. STEPHEN G. OPPERWALL, Esq. of Law Offices of Stephen G. Opperwall (Tel: 925-417-0300) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. JONATHAN RYAN TUNG, Esq. of Law Offices of Jonathan Ryan Tung (Tel: 408-359-7311) specially appearing with/for defendant YESSICA CAI and for defendant Yifeng Chen.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS YESSICA CAI, YIFENG CHEN's Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Or Default and Default Judgment, If Entered Under CCP 473 (B), CCP 473.5, CCP 473(D) For Extrinsic Fraud Or Mistake And Quash Service Of Summons (CCP 418.10).

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Yessica Cai and Yifeng Chen's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The motion was filed six weeks after default judgment, and California courts have a strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) The grounds for grant are statutory (CCP 473 at a minimum) and in the court's inherent equitable powers. (Sporn v. Home Depot USA, Inc. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1294, 1300.) Defendants are in pro per and were confused by the pro per complaint. Relief is available for "extrinsic mistake," with that term given broad interpretation. (In re Marriage of Park (1980) 27 Cal.3d 337, 342.)

Counsel for defendants to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendant Yessica Cai and Yifeng Chen.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Plaintiff Howard Lee was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Yessica Cai or Yifeng Chen.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Promissory Fraud, Negligent Misrepresentation, Unjust Enrichment, Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress and Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress was filed February 2, 2021, and personally served on defendant Yessica Cai on march 5, 2021.
Substitute service on defendant Yifeng Chen occurred on March 5, 2021 and was effectuated on March 15, 2021.
Default as to defendants Yessica Cai and Yifeng Chen was entered on April 27, 2021.
Dismissal Without Prejudice as to defendants NYCA, LLC and Carmencita Jocson was filed with the Clerk's Office on August 9, 2021.
No DOE defendants were named in plaintiff's complaint.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Howard Lee, and against the defaulted defendants Yessica CA and Yifeng Chen, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $1,150,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $3,148,597.87.
Costs - $762.48.
Total - $4,299,360.35.

Plaintiff to prepare proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
